name: Crab Nebula
description: Remnant of a supernova observed in 1054 AD by Chinese astronomers. Contains a rapidly spinning neutron star (pulsar) at its center.

name: Hercules Cluster
description: One of the brightest globular clusters visible from the Northern Hemisphere. Contains about 300,000 stars packed into a sphere 145 light-years across.

name: Andromeda Galaxy
description: The nearest major galaxy to the Milky Way. Contains approximately 1 trillion stars. Will collide with our galaxy in about 4.5 billion years.

name: Orion Nebula
description: One of the brightest nebulae, visible to the naked eye. A stellar nursery where new stars are being born right now.

name: Pleiades
description: The 'Seven Sisters' — one of the nearest and most obvious star clusters to Earth. Contains over 1,000 confirmed members.

name: Whirlpool Galaxy
description: A classic grand-design spiral galaxy interacting with its smaller companion NGC 5195. First galaxy whose spiral structure was discovered.

name: Ring Nebula
description: A glowing shell of ionized gas expelled by a dying red giant star. The central white dwarf star has a surface temperature of 120,000 K.

name: Bode's Galaxy
description: A grand design spiral galaxy about the size of the Milky Way. Its proximity and active galactic nucleus make it a favorite target for astronomers.

name: Virgo A
description: A supergiant elliptical galaxy. Its supermassive black hole was the first ever directly imaged by the Event Horizon Telescope in 2019.

name: Sombrero Galaxy
description: Named for its resemblance to a Mexican hat. Features a bright nucleus, large central bulge, and prominent dust lane in its outer disk.

name: Rosette Nebula
description: A large spherical H II region (emission nebula) resembling a rose. The open cluster NGC 2244 at its center provides the UV radiation that ionizes the gas.

name: Cat's Eye Nebula
description: One of the most complex planetary nebulae known, with remarkable concentric gas shells, jets, and knots of gas.

name: IC 1101
description: One of the largest known galaxies in the observable universe, spanning about 4 million light-years. Contains over 100 trillion stars.

name: Sagittarius A*
description: The supermassive black hole at the center of our Milky Way galaxy. Its mass is approximately 4 million times that of the Sun. Imaged by EHT in 2022.

name: Pillars of Creation
description: Iconic columns of interstellar gas and dust in the Eagle Nebula (M16). Famously photographed by Hubble in 1995 and JWST in 2022.
